methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $105M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Genesis AI, a Seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Zhou Xian and Theophile Gervet, has raised $105M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a universe of 9 private peers (including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics) and 1 public comparable in the Robotics & Manufacturing sector.

Stage-progression methodology applies a 2.5x multiplier to the $105M capital raised proxy, reflecting typical Seed-to-next-stage value creation. This yields an implied valuation of $262M. Peer-median analysis was not applicable; while 11 same-sector peers exist, fewer than three meet narrow same-stage or adjacent-stage filters, making a sector-wide median unreliable without introducing material bias. No triangulation across multiple methods was therefore possible.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $223M (low) to $302M (high), with a median point of $262M. Key uncertainties include the absence of narrow-stage comparable data and the reliance on a single methodological approach. Refinement would benefit from either additional Seed-stage robotics peers or greater specificity around Genesis AI's revenue run rate, unit economics, or technical differentiation relative to the identified peer set.
